title: Cenostigma marginatum
taxonomy: Cenostigma marginatum is classified within the family Fabaceae. This classification places it among the legume family, which includes a wide variety of flowering plants. The genus Cenostigma is considered part of the subfamily Faboideae. This subfamily comprises many species of legumes that are known for their distinctive flowers and seed pods. As part of the Fabaceae family, Cenostigma marginatum shares characteristics with other legume species.

intro: Cenostigma marginatum, also known as Brazilian guanabira, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of South America, belonging to the family Fabaceae. The health benefits of Cenostigma marginatum include its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in treating various conditions such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Cenostigma marginatum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
